chapter_title.ar: باب مَا يَجُوزُ مِنْ شَهَادَةِ النِّسَاءِ وَ مَا لَا يَجُوزُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 13 – What is allowed from the testimony of the women, and what is not allowed

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/238)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/238)
text: قُلْنَا أَ تَجُوزُ شَهَادَةُ النِّسَاءِ فِي الْحُدُودِ فَقَالَ فِي الْقَتْلِ وَحْدَهُ إِنَّ عَلِيّاً ( عليه السلام ) كَانَ يَقُولُ لَا يَبْطُلُ دَمُ امْرِئٍ مُسْلِمٍ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Jameel Bin Darraj and Muhammad Bin Humran,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws, said, ‘We said, ‘Is a testimony of the women allowed regarding the legal penalties (Hadd)?’ So heasws said: ‘Regarding the killing alone. Aliasws was saying: ‘The blood of a Muslim person would not be invalidated’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Jamil ibn Darraj and Muhammad ibn Humran who has narrated the following: “We asked abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, if testimony of women is acceptable in penalty cases. He (the Imam) said, ‘Only in murder cases it is acceptable. ’Amir al-Mu’minin, Ali, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, would say, “The blood (life) of a Muslim cannot be invalidated.’””
